$comment: Base model whitelist for forge-architect. User-editable. Each entry has the HF repo id, parameter count, license, recommended chat template, and which forge regimes are viable. forge-architect filters by license+language, scores by domain fit + size delta, and selects.

models.notes: Will be distilled / pruned to ≤300M for the forge ceiling.
Distill-only. Strong at reasoning at small sizes.
Apache-2.0 — preferred when license_preference is permissive. Prunes cleanly to 280M.
Apache-2.0. LoRA-SFT viable up to 300M target via low-rank adapters.
Apache-2.0. Instruction-tuned base — LoRA on top compounds a trained instruction-follower. Preferred over Qwen2.5-1.5B (base) for Q/A SFT.

Qwen Research License (free for research, non-commercial default; check terms for commercial use). ~10 GB GPU mem for fp16 LoRA — fits 24 GB (A10G/L4). Sweet spot for domain-SFT with moderate corpora.
Apache-2.0. QLoRA-only on 24 GB GPUs (A10G/L4) — fp16 LoRA won't fit. 4-bit NF4 base + bf16 LoRA fits in ~12 GB, leaves headroom for seq_len=2048. The clinical-reasoning quality tier on this infra.
Apache-2.0. Chat-tuned base, smaller compute footprint.
Tokenizer trained from corpus. Custom 50M-300M architecture (decoder-only Transformer, configurable depth/width). Use only when corpus ≥10B tokens AND user accepts longer training.
